channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A new wearable from dating app RAW promises to track your partner’s emotions in real time
item.description: Forget a diamond ring, the latest symbol of commitment now comes in the form of wearable tech. The RAW ring, created by the dating app RAW and Queens Tech, allows couples to track each other’s emotions, both good and bad.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Samsung's Android XR headset could avoid the Apple Vision Pro's biggest mistake, according to this leak
item.description: Samsung is set to release first-party Android XR controllers, and that’s the best news about its headset so far – here’s why.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: Multicloud: Tips for getting it right
item.description: More and more companies are adopting a multicloud strategy. The goal? To maximize the flexibility and performance of their IT landscapes. They combine cloud services from different providers in a targeted way to exploit the advantages of different platforms.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OpenAI's New ChatGPT Model Sparks Debate on AI Writing
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman shares a metafiction short story written by AI, showcasing the company's new creative writing model, but raises questions about its potential impact on human writers.
item.dc:creator: john-anthony.disotto@futurenet.com (John-Anthony Disotto), TechRadar

item.title: Hitman: World of Assassination hits PSVR 2 soon, finally giving you a reason to dust off your headset
item.description: VR Games Showcase showed off 20 new Meta Quest games, plus gave us a release date for Hitman: World of Assassination on PSVR 2.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft’s plan for genAI profits: Squeeze customers
item.description: Microsoft has ridden its multibillion-dollar investments in generative AI (genAI) to become the world’s second-most valuable company, with a valuation of roughly $3 trillion, depending on the day’s stock price.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What comes after search? Cloudflare’s CEO says that’s an ‘existential’ question for the internet
item.description: Google has made it much easier to find the answers we seek without navigating to various websites, but that has made it much harder to do business for media companies and other creators.
item.dc:creator: Anna-Louise Jackson, Fast Company

item.title: Musk outlines DOGE work in rare TV interview
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk offered new details about the work of President Trump’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) on Monday, while doubling down on the group’s efforts amid mounting criticism.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: OPM inspector general investigating DOGE emails, access to agency data
item.description: The inspector general for the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) told lawmakers he is investigating the use of a new data system at the agency that has been part of a broader effort by Elon Musk and his allies to email federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: X outage affects thousands of users
item.description: An outage on the social platform X appeared to impact thousands of users on Monday morning. According to outage-tracking website Downdetector.com, more than 37,000 users reported problems with the platform shortly before 10 a.m. EDT on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Guatemala's Volcano of Fire erupts and forces evacuations
item.description: Guatemala’s Volcano of Fire is erupting, and authorities have evacuated nearly 300 families while warning that another 30,000 people in the area could be at risk.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Elon Musk says X outages caused by cyberattack
item.description: Musk hypothesized on his platform that "Either a large, coordinated group and/or a country" were responsible.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Databricks’ new updates aim to ease gen AI app and agent development
item.description: Data lakehouse provider Databricks is introducing four new updates to its portfolio to help enterprises have more control over the development of their agents and other generative AI-based applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: iDEAL Semiconductor CEO decries CHIPS Act loss, warns of tech talent gap
item.description: In his address to Congress last week, US President Donald J. Trump renewed his criticism of the CHIPS Act, the initiative aimed at reshoring the semiconductor industry, calling it “horrible.” He also called for any remaining funds to be returned to taxpayers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New delivery robot can haul 2,200 pounds of your stuff
item.description: WeRide's unmanned electric delivery van, the Robovan W5, has 194 cubic feet of cargo space and can carry payloads up to about 2,205 pounds
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft previews AI chat template for .NET
item.description: Microsoft is previewing a .NET web app template intended to enable quick development of AI-powered chat applications. Part of an ongoing effort to make AI development with .NET easier to discover and use, the AI Chat Web App template was announced March 6.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chinese humanoid robot lands world's first front flip
item.description: A robotics firm in China claims a robot has performed the world's first humanoid robot front flip, which is significantly more difficult than a backflip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride
item.description: This blog will focus on requirements in the context of the federal government. Sound requirements drive best value pricing and performance for customer agencies The post Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Anthropic’s upgraded Console targets more collaboration among developers
item.description: Anthropic has upgraded its Console — an interface that lets enterprises create API keys, add more users to a team, set up billing, and experiment with Claude on the Workbench, in an effort to promote more collaboration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Alibaba says its new AI model rivals DeepSeeks’s R-1, OpenAI’s o1
item.description: Alibaba Cloud on Thursday launched QwQ-32B, a compact reasoning model built on its latest large language model (LLM), Qwen2.5-32b, one it says delivers performance comparable to other large cutting edge models, including Chinese rival DeepSeek and OpenAI’s o1, with only 32 billion parameters.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Achieving unified visibility for effective zero trust implementation
item.description: All components of a zero trust architecture must generate data that is observable, available and able to be correlated within a unified data plane. The post Achieving unified visibility for effective zero trust implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Seven important trends in the server sphere
item.description: The pace of change around server technology is advancing considerably, driven by hyperscalers but spilling over into the on-premises world as well.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: The two features each work to identify and warn you of a possible scam while it's unfolding, either over text or on a phone call.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, NewsNation
